findings.title: No script measures the tokens/s speedup ratios reported in Fig. 6 and Table 2
findings.claim: stream_generate / ea_generate yield per-step accepted_tokens, but no script in the repo times generation or aggregates tokens-per-second into the speedup ratios reported in Fig. 6 (per-model speedups) or Table 2 (Medusa-extension 1.83x..2.43x).
findings.concern: The headline efficiency claims (up to 4x faster, +22% over MTP, Table 2 ratios) cannot be reproduced from the repo because the timing/aggregation harness that produces them is absent.
findings.resolution: Authors: please add the driver that runs generation under each paradigm, measures wall-clock tokens/s, and computes the reported speedup ratios.
findings.reason: The quote at model.py:432-440 is confirmed; a repo-wide search for speedup/tokens_per_second/perf_counter outside LLaMA-Factory found no timing harness that aggregates wall-clock tok/s into the ratios reported in Fig. 6 and Table 2.

findings.title: Top-level L-MTP package (models/, eval/) ships no dependency specification
findings.quote:
import transformers
from .head import Head
from safetensors.torch import save_file, load_file
import os
from typing import Callable, Optional, Tuple, Union

from ..base import AutoModel
import torch
import torch.nn as nn

from ..base.kv_cache import initialize_past_key_values
from transformers.generation.utils import GenerateDecoderOnlyOutput, CausalLMOutputWithPast
from ..utils import prepare_logits_processor, reset_tree_mode, reset_past_key_values,  evaluate_posterior, generate_candidates
from ..utils import initialize_tree, tree_decoding, generate_tree_buffers, update_inference_inputs
from ..utils_eg import initialize_tree as initialize_tree_eagle, tree_decoding as tree_decoding_eagle, update_inference_inputs as update_inference_inputs_eagle

from peft import PeftModel, PeftConfig

from transformers.modeling_utils import PreTrainedModel
from .config import LMTPConfig

from transformers.loss.loss_utils import ForCausalLMLoss
findings.claim: The repo root has no requirements.txt / environment.yml for the custom L-MTP code, which depends on safetensors, peft, fastchat, fire, networkx, and an unspecified transformers version exposing transformers.loss.loss_utils.ForCausalLMLoss; only LLaMA-Factory has its own requirements.txt.
findings.concern: Without a pinned environment for the custom code (notably the exact transformers version that exposes transformers.loss.loss_utils), the L-MTP model is not guaranteed to import/run on a fresh machine.
findings.resolution: Authors: provide a requirements/lock file (or pin transformers) for the top-level models/ and eval/ code.
findings.reason: Quote at model.py:1-22 confirmed exactly; ls of the repo root shows no requirements.txt or environment.yml (only LLaMA-Factory/ has requirements.txt and setup.py), confirming the absence claim.

findings.title: models/__init__.py and eval scripts import a models/medusa module that does not exist
findings.quote:
from .lmtp.model import LMTPModel
from .medusa.model import MedusaModel
from .medusa.model_official import MedusaModel as MedusaModelOfficial
from .vanilla.model import Vanilla
findings.claim: The package entrypoint imports from .medusa.model and .medusa.model_official, but models/medusa/ is absent from the repo (git ls-files lists no medusa file; only lmtp, vanilla, base under models/). eval/get_all.py:5 and eval/plot_tree.py:5 import the same missing module.
findings.concern: Any `import models` / `from models import get_mtp_model` — required by LLaMA-Factory training (loader.py:171) and by the head-accuracy eval (get_all.py) — raises ModuleNotFoundError, so neither training nor head-accuracy evaluation runs as shipped.
findings.resolution: Authors: add the missing models/medusa/ package or remove the dead medusa imports from models/__init__.py, eval/get_all.py, and eval/plot_tree.py.
findings.reason: Quote at models/__init__.py:3-6 confirmed exactly; ls of models/ shows only lmtp, vanilla, base directories — medusa/ is absent, confirming a ModuleNotFoundError on import.

findings.title: LLaMA-Factory training integration hardcodes /home/storage/LMTP on sys.path
findings.quote:
if model_args.mtp_type is not None and model_args.mtp_type != "vanilla":
    import sys
    sys.path.append(os.path.join("/home/storage/LMTP"))
    from models import get_mtp_model

    model = get_mtp_model(
        model_args.mtp_type,
        model_args.model_name_or_path,
        tokenizer,
        model,
        train_mode=is_trainable,
        num_head=model_args.num_heads,
        head_weight=model_args.head_weight,
        stage1_pretrained_path=model_args.stage1_pretrained_path,
        train_lm_head=model_args.train_lm_head
    )
findings.claim: The only place that wires L-MTP into LLaMA-Factory training appends a hardcoded absolute path '/home/storage/LMTP' to sys.path to locate the `models` package, and every stage-1/stage-2 config sets stage1_pretrained_path under '/home/storage/LMTP/saves/...'.
findings.concern: On any machine where the repo is not checked out at /home/storage/LMTP, `from models import get_mtp_model` fails (or imports nothing), so the documented training commands (README) do not run as shipped.
findings.resolution: Authors: make the path configurable/relative (e.g. derive from repo root or an env var) and parameterise the stage1_pretrained_path defaults in the configs.
findings.reason: Quote at loader.py:168-183 confirmed exactly, including sys.path.append('/home/storage/LMTP'); the config at configs/s2/qwen25_7B_base_lmtp_stage2.yaml:7 also shows the hardcoded stage1_pretrained_path under /home/storage/LMTP/saves/.

findings.title: Code hardcodes leap stride skip_token=3; paper default is k=2
findings.quote:
skip = self.skip_token
for i, logits_ in enumerate(logits):
    h_logits = logits_[:, : -(skip*(i+1))].contiguous()
    h_labels = labels[..., skip*(i+1) :].contiguous()
    loss_i = self.loss_fct(logits=h_logits, labels=h_labels, vocab_size=self.config.vocab_size, **kwargs)
    loss += loss_i * alpha
findings.claim: loss_function uses self.skip_token as the leap stride; self.skip_token is hardcoded to 3 at model.py:128 (`self.skip_token = self.config.skip_token = 3`) and is never overridden by any config (configs set only mtp_type/num_heads/head_weight) or by get_mtp_model. The paper states 'We set k = 2 and n = 4 by default' (Section 5.1).
findings.concern: The shipped default trains additional heads at offsets {3,6,9} (skip=3) rather than the paper's k=2 leaping pattern, so re-running the configs as-is does not reproduce the paper's default L-MTP setting; the head's leap positions differ from Eq. (4)/(5) with k=2.
findings.resolution: Authors: confirm which k was used for the reported Table 1 numbers, and expose skip_token (k) as a config field instead of hardcoding 3 in LMTPModel.__init__.
findings.paper_ref: Section 5.1 (Implementation details): 'We set k = 2 and n = 4 by default.'
findings.reason: model.py:128 confirms `self.skip_token = self.config.skip_token = 3` (hardcoded); model.py:219-224 confirms the loss loop uses skip*(i+1); no config or get_mtp_model call overrides skip_token, contradicting the paper's stated k=2 default.

findings.title: All training configs set num_heads: 3; paper default is n = 4
findings.claim: Every stage-1 and stage-2 config in configs/ sets num_heads: 3 (verified across all 12 yaml files), whereas the paper's default is n = 4 (Section 5.1). The eval drivers (eval/get_all.py, eval/head_accuracy.py) also default num_head=3.
findings.concern: The shipped configs do not match the paper's default n=4 setting used for the headline Table 1 numbers, so the provided configs reproduce a different (n=3) configuration than reported.
findings.resolution: Authors: clarify whether Table 1 used n=4 (paper) or n=3 (configs), and ship configs matching the reported default.
findings.paper_ref: Section 5.1 (Implementation details): 'We set k = 2 and n = 4 by default.'
findings.reason: Quote at configs/s2/qwen25_7B_base_lmtp_stage2.yaml:4-6 confirmed exactly with num_heads: 3, contradicting the paper's stated n=4 default.

findings.title: Additional heads trained at offsets k, 2k, 3k rather than paper's k+1, 2k+1, 3k+1
findings.claim: The additional heads (0-indexed i) are supervised on tokens at offset skip*(i+1) ahead, i.e. {k, 2k, 3k}. The paper's Eq.(4)/(5) place leaping heads at t+k(i-1)+1, i.e. additional-head offsets {k+1, 2k+1, 3k+1}; for k=2 these are {3,5,7} but the code (even with skip=2) supervises {2,4,6}.
findings.concern: The implemented leap positions are uniformly shifted by one token relative to the paper's stated formula; both are valid 'leap' patterns, so this is a faithfulness mismatch rather than an invalid procedure, but it means the trained heads do not target the exact positions described.
findings.resolution: Authors: confirm the intended leap offsets and reconcile the code's skip*(i+1) indexing with Eq.(4)/(5)'s t+k(i-1)+1.
findings.reason: Quote at model.py:220-222 confirmed exactly; the skip*(i+1) offset arithmetic is correct as described — this is a genuine paper/code discrepancy in leap position indexing, though it is a faithfulness question rather than a functional bug.
